page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC-Rome processors running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 16 physical cores with a single thread per core and a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled directly with the instance, offering 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2912.97, placing it in the top 25% of tested instances. However, its multi-core performance is more moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 46204.81, falling into the middle 50% average category. This performance profile is influenced by the shared CPU allocation model, which may introduce resource contention under sustained multi-threaded workloads, though single-threaded execution remains highly competitive.

Given its high memory density of 128.0 GB and substantial 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for workloads requiring large memory footprints and fast local storage without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. The combination of high capacity local NVMe storage and generous RAM makes it appropriate for database hosting, caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from fast disk I/O but do not require sustained, non-shared multi-core CPU performance.
description: The Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is a memory-optimized instance from the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs on the AMD EPYC-Rome architecture with a shared CPU allocation. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and a substantial 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In performance benchmarks, the server delivers strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2912.97 in stress-ng tests, while its multi-core performance of 46204.81 is average. This resource configuration makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy and storage-intensive applications. It is appropriate for database hosting, caching systems, and data processing tasks that require significant memory and fast local storage but can tolerate a shared CPU allocation.

meta_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and strong single-core performance for data-intensive workloads.
